item.title: AT&T signs deal worth $2 billion to upgrade emergency cellular network
item.description: AT&T has reached a deal to invest about $1 billion to ​improve the Commerce Department's FirstNet ‌and deliver $1 billion in cost savings for the program via reduced rates, a U.S ​government agency said on Tuesday.

item.title: Exclusive: Investor Artisan Partners backs Unilever's plan to sell food unit
item.description: Artisan Partners, ​which has a history of driving strategic change at consumer companies, welcomed Unilever's decision on Tuesday to merge its ‌food business with McCormick , saying the Dove soap maker should now be able to "more effectively manage" its core personal care and home brands.

item.title: X down for thousands of users in the US, Downdetector shows
item.description: Social media platform ​X was down ‌for thousands of users ​in ​the U.S. on Tuesday, ⁠according ​to Downdetector.com.

item.title: Trading Day: Q1 ends with a BANG!
item.description: Wall Street closed a tumultuous first quarter with a bang on Tuesday as hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East powered stocks to their best day in almost a year, even as data showed a slump in U.S. job openings and hirings.

item.title: Beyond Meat misses quarterly results estimates on persistent demand struggles
item.description: Faux meat maker Beyond Meat missed Wall Street estimates for fourth-quarter sales and posted a larger-than-expected operating loss on Tuesday due ​to sluggish demand for its higher-priced processed food products.

item.title: NY Fed appoints Citi CEO Jane Fraser to Federal Advisory Council
item.description: The Federal Reserve Bank of New ​York said on Tuesday it has appointed Jane ‌Fraser, chief executive of Citigroup , to the Federal Reserve Board of Governors' Advisory Council.

item.title: Elon Musk must face class action over late disclosure of Twitter stake, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday said former Twitter investors ‌who accused Elon Musk of defrauding them by waiting too long to disclose his initial investment in the social media company may pursue their case as a class action.

item.title: North Korea-linked hack hits largely invisible software that powers online services
item.description: Hackers linked to North Korea breached behind-the-scenes software that runs many common online functions in an effort to steal login information that could enable further cyber operations, ​Google said on Tuesday.

item.title: Nike results top estimates as turnaround shows uneven progress
item.description: Nike beat expectations for third-quarter earnings on Tuesday, as the sportswear giant's turnaround efforts showed some signs of progress, particularly in ​its wholesale business, even as weakness in China and margin pressure ‌persisted.

item.title: MercadoLibre's fintech terminates its cryptocurrency Mercado Coin
item.description: Mercado Pago, the fintech arm of e-commerce ​firm MercadoLibre , said on Tuesday ‌it is discontinuing its cryptocurrency, called Mercado Coin, which it had launched ​in 2022 as part of ​its loyalty program.

item.title: US customs agency says tariff refund system progressing, but payments may take up to 45 days
item.description: The U.S. customs agency said on Tuesday it was making progress in setting up a streamlined process for refunding some $166 billion in tariff collections deemed illegal ​by the Supreme Court, but that its new system could take up to ‌45 days to review and process refund applications.

item.title: Monzo to close US business to focus on UK and Europe
item.description: British ‌mobile bank Monzo said on Tuesday it is exiting the United States, describing ​the move as a strategic ​decision to focus on expanding in ⁠the UK and Europe.

item.title: Exclusive: TikTok seeks Brazil fintech license to offer credit
item.description: Social media platform TikTok, controlled by China's ByteDance, is seeking approval from the Brazilian central bank to operate as a lending and ​payments fintech, according to two people with direct knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Oracle begins cutting thousands of jobs, CNBC reports
item.description: Cloud computing company Oracle is laying off thousands of employees, CNBC reported on Tuesday, citing two ​people familiar with the matter.

item.title: German automaker Mercedes-Benz to invest $4 billion in Alabama SUV plant
item.description: German automaker Mercedes-Benz said on Tuesday it ​will invest $4 billion at its Alabama plant ‌through 2030 to boost SUV production as it seeks to address significant U.S. auto tariffs.

item.title: Unilever and McCormick investors find $65 billion food deal hard to swallow
item.description: Unilever and McCormick shareholders found the reality of their planned $65 billion ​food deal hard to digest on Tuesday, perturbed by the transaction's structure, its long timeline to closing and the potential for antitrust scrutiny.

item.title: Activist investor Irenic swoops in on Snap with new stake, shares surge
item.description: Activist investor Irenic Capital Management said on Tuesday that technology company Snap could ​be worth at least five times as much as today's valuation but ‌needs to cut costs, optimize its portfolio and use artificial intelligence better.

item.title: Apple tests Siri feature that handles multiple commands at once, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Apple is testing a ​feature that would let Siri process multiple requests in ‌a single query, bringing the virtual assistant more in line with the capabilities of newer AI assistants, Bloomberg News reported on ​Tuesday.

item.title: WTO cannot address trade imbalances, but US not ready to exit, Greer says
item.description: The World Trade Organization is unable to deal with the core challenges of the global trading system, but the ​U.S. is not ready to leave the body, U.S. Trade ‌Representative Jamieson Greer said on Tuesday.

item.title: KKR plans to privatise Japan's Taiyo Holdings with $3.3 billion tender offer
item.description: Global investment firm KKR said ​on Tuesday that an entity owned ‌by its investment funds seeks to make a tender offer to buy all shares of Japanese chemical ​manufacturer Taiyo Holdings for 528.56 billion ​yen ($3.33 billion).

item.title: Warren Buffett will not commit to more donations to Gates Foundation
item.description: Warren Buffett on Tuesday declined to commit to continuing his annual ​multibillion-dollar donations to the Gates Foundation, following the recent release of materials about late sex offender ‌Jeffrey Epstein's relationship with the nonprofit and its co-founder Bill Gates.

item.title: US job openings fall in February; hiring lowest since pandemic
item.description: U.S. job openings fell more than expected in February and hiring dropped to the lowest ​level in nearly six years, government data showed ‌on Tuesday.

item.title: Tether cuts two gold traders hired three months ago, source says
item.description: Tether has let go two senior precious metals traders it hired from HSBC ​only three months ago, a source with direct knowledge of ‌the matter told Reuters on Tuesday.

item.title: NTSB raises questions on driver assistance systems, lack of regulations
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board ​on Tuesday criticized ‌the lack of government regulations on driver ​assistance systems and ​raised questions about claims ⁠made by automakers.

item.title: US consumer confidence nudges higher in March
item.description: U.S. consumer confidence unexpectedly edged up in March, but households anticipated ​higher inflation over the next 12 ‌months amid a surge in gasoline prices and continued tariffs pass-through, a survey showed on ​Tuesday.

item.title: Meta to limit PG-13 rating use for teen accounts in Motion Picture Association deal
item.description: Meta has ​agreed to limit references to the ‌PG-13 film rating when describing accounts for teens as part of a deal between ​the Motion Picture Association and ​the company, Meta and the MPA ⁠said in a statement.

item.title: AI-led selloff in contract research firms may be misjudging disruption risk
item.description: Shares of contract research organizations have tumbled on fears that advances in artificial intelligence could allow drugmakers to take clinical trial work in-house, but industry experts say the selloff overestimates how far the technology can replace the sector's core capabilities.

item.title: Meta unveils two Ray-Ban smart glasses for prescription wearers starting at $499
item.description: Meta Platforms launched two new Ray-Ban prescription smart ​glasses on Tuesday, expanding its offerings in ‌an area that has become one of the few breakthrough successes in the race for artificial intelligence-powered gadgets.

item.title: CoreWeave secures $8.5 billion loan to expand AI infrastructure
item.description: Cloud infrastructure firm CoreWeave has secured $8.5 billion in financing through ​a delayed-draw term loan facility ‌to expand its artificial intelligence cloud platform, as demand for computing power continues ​to surge.

item.title: US single-family home prices rise moderately in January, FHFA says
item.description: U.S. single-family house prices increased moderately in January, but rising mortgage rates as the war in the Middle East ​drags on could sideline potential first-time buyers from the housing ‌market.
